_metadata.use_case:
I need help analyzing public comments submitted to a federal regulatory docket regarding medical payment products (medical credit cards and loans). These comments were submitted in response to a joint Request for Information (RFI) from the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB), Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), and Department of the Treasury.

The comments come from diverse stakeholders:
- Individual patients sharing personal experiences with medical credit cards
- Healthcare

description: Example schema instance for response analysis fields

enum_fields.hint: Classify the stakeholder as one of: private individual, organization, or government agency

enum_fields.description: Advocacy orientation of the commenting organization
enum_fields.hint: Classify the organization's advocacy type. Use 'not_applicable' when stakeholder_type is 'private_individual'.

categorical_fields.description: Factors the commenter identifies as driving the impact
categorical_fields.hint: Select one or more drivers that the commenter identifies as causing the impact they assert has occurred or will occur. Identify only what the commenter blames or credits. Do not infer whether the impact is positive or negative—that is captured in impact_sentiment. Use 'unclear' only when the commenter does not specify a driver.

categorical_fields.description: Types of vulnerable populations mentioned in the comment, including the commenter.
categorical_fields.hint: Select the vulnerable population tags that are explicitly or implicitly mentioned. If no vulnerable populations are mentioned, leave empty.

categorical_fields.description: Types of harmful impacts mentioned in the comment.
categorical_fields.hint: Select all harmful impacts explicitly mentioned by the commenter or clearly implied by their described experiences. Include only impacts the commenter reports or clearly experienced, not inferred downstream consequences. Use 'None or Not Applicable' if no harmful impacts are described.

categorical_fields.description: Types of beneficial impacts mentioned in the comment.
categorical_fields.hint: Select all beneficial impacts explicitly mentioned by the commenter or clearly implied by their described experiences. Include only impacts the commenter reports or clearly experienced, not inferred downstream consequences. Use 'None or Not Applicable' if no beneficial impacts are described.

categorical_fields.description: Product names or company mentions referenced in the comment.
categorical_fields.hint: Select known products or companies when clearly mentioned; use 'None or Not Applicable' when no explicit mention exists.

categorical_fields.description: Primary role of the commenter in relation to medical payment products
categorical_fields.hint: Choose the single option that best describes the submitter's primary role or affiliation. Use 'other' when none fit precisely. This field is single-select for visualization as an enum-like category.

categorical_fields.description: Commenter's overall stance toward medical payment products
categorical_fields.hint: Select the single option that best captures whether the commenter endorses, is neutral about, expresses mixed views, or opposes the products.

categorical_fields.description: Vulnerable populations the commenter identifies as belonging to or representing
categorical_fields.hint: Select one or more vulnerable population tags explicitly mentioned by the commenter. May be empty if not referenced.

categorical_fields.description: Named financial products, card brands, or companies referenced in the comment
categorical_fields.hint: Select all explicitly named products or companies. Use 'Other Named Product/Company' when a named party is not in the suggested list; use 'None or Not Applicable' when no specific product is mentioned.

categorical_fields.description: Types of regulatory or policy actions the commenter recommends
categorical_fields.hint: Select one or more regulatory actions advocated by the commenter. Use 'other_policy_action' for suggestions that do not fit the listed categories.

categorical_fields.description: High-level RFI topics or specific question areas the comment addresses
categorical_fields.hint: Tag which RFI topics or question buckets the comment addresses to enable cross-topic analysis.

categorical_fields.description: Downstream impacts referenced in the comment
categorical_fields.hint: Select all downstream impacts explicitly mentioned or clearly implied by the commenter.

categorical_fields.description: Primary emotional tone expressed in the narrative text
categorical_fields.hint: Tag one or more dominant emotions conveyed by the commenter; multiple tags allowed when mixed emotions are present.

scalar_fields.description: Magnitude of impact asserted by the commenter
scalar_fields.hint: While keeping in mind that impact can be good, bad, unknown, etc., rate the overall amount of impact the commenter asserts. This asserted impact may have already happened, or it may be contingent on a future event or decision. Use a 0-10 scale where 0 means no impact and 10 means the strongest possible impact. If the commenter does not assert any impact, score 0.

scalar_fields.description: Direction of the asserted impact from the commenter's perspective (harmful to beneficial)
scalar_fields.scale_interpretation: 0 = most harmful impact, 10 = most beneficial impact
scalar_fields.hint: Rate the direction of impact from the commenter's perspective, where zero indicates the most harmful impact and 10 indicates the most beneficial impact. Consider the commenter's perspective when rating. If the commenter describes a mix of positive and negative impacts, rate the net sentiment.

scalar_fields.description: Time-sensitivity and urgency of the issue for decision-making or action
scalar_fields.scale_interpretation: 0 = low urgency, tangential to core issues, 10 = high urgency, requires immediate attention or decision
scalar_fields.hint: Rate how time-sensitive or urgent the commenter's concerns are. 10 means the issue requires immediate attention or action because a decision will have imminent consequences (good or bad). 0 means the comment is tangential to core issues, low-priority, or the commenter is primarily advertising their own business rather than addressing the substantive policy question. Consider whether the commenter is raising a concern that policymakers must address urgently versus making a general point or tangential argument.

scalar_fields.description: Net sentiment toward medical payment products from the commenter's perspective
scalar_fields.scale_interpretation: 0 = strongly negative toward products, 10 = strongly positive/supportive of products
scalar_fields.hint: Rate how positive or negative the commenter is about medical payment products overall. Consider explicit praise, criticism, or conditional support. 0 means most negative; 10 means most positive.

scalar_fields.description: How time-sensitive or urgent the commenter frames their concerns
scalar_fields.scale_interpretation: 0 = low urgency or informational, 10 = immediate urgency requiring regulatory/actionable response
scalar_fields.hint: Rate urgency of the issues raised for policymakers or stakeholders. 10 indicates immediate action is needed.

scalar_fields.description: Severity of life impact described (health, housing, employment, basic needs)
scalar_fields.scale_interpretation: 0 = no material life impact, 10 = highest severity (life-threatening, loss of housing, inability to obtain essential care)
scalar_fields.hint: Score how severely the comment suggests the product impacted the individual's or community's life outcomes.

scalar_fields.description: Magnitude of financial harm or risk described resulting from the product
scalar_fields.scale_interpretation: 0 = no financial harm, 10 = catastrophic financial harm (bankruptcy, loss of home, severe credit impairment)
scalar_fields.hint: Consider reported harms such as collections, credit score damage, garnishments, or bankruptcy when scoring.

scalar_fields.description: Degree to which the product improved access to needed care for the commenter
scalar_fields.scale_interpretation: 0 = no access benefit, 10 = enabled essential care that would not otherwise have occurred
scalar_fields.hint: Score the extent the commenter credits the product with facilitating access to care (e.g., allowed procedure otherwise unaffordable).

scalar_fields.description: Intensity of emotional language or sentiment in the personal narrative (if present)
scalar_fields.hint: Rate how emotionally charged the commenter's narrative is, independent of positive/negative valence.

key_quotes_fields.description: Quotes capturing concrete examples of lived experience with harms or benefits
key_quotes_fields.hint: Select up to 3 direct quotes that capture concrete examples of lived experience with harms or benefits from the commenter's perspective. Prioritize authentic narratives and specific experiences that illustrate the impact. May be empty if the comment lacks substantive narrative content or lived experience examples.

text_array_fields.description: Name or names of individual commenters
text_array_fields.hint: Extract one string per individual commenter identified in the comment. May be empty if no individual names are provided.

text_array_fields.description: Name or names of the organizations commenting
text_array_fields.hint: Extract one string per organization identified in the comment. May be empty if no organization names are provided.

text_array_fields.description: Causal or conditional arguments extracted from the comment
text_array_fields.hint: Extract claims that connect causes to consequences (e.g., 'if X then Y', 'because X, therefore Y'). Focus on arguments relevant to the commenter's impact_drivers, amount_of_impact, and impact_sentiment. May be empty if no clear arguments are present.

text_array_fields.description: Advice, desires, wishes, or policy recommendations mentioned in the comment.
text_array_fields.hint: Extract policy recommendations as short phrases; use 'None or Not Applicable' or 'No Change/Maintain Status Quo' when applicable.

text_array_fields.description: Up to three short direct quotes or paraphrases that capture the central lived-experience or emotional core of the comment
text_array_fields.hint: Extract up to 3 brief passages (sentences or short paragraphs) that best illustrate personal impact, emotion, or a compelling example. May be empty if absent.

text_array_fields.description: Person names identified in the submission (submitter, referenced individuals)
text_array_fields.hint: List one string per individual name found in the comment. Leave empty if none.

text_array_fields.description: Organization names (employers, advocacy groups, hospitals, vendors) mentioned
text_array_fields.hint: Extract organization names to support aggregation (e.g., hospitals, nonprofits, companies).

text_array_fields.description: Named financial product brands, credit card networks, platform providers, or merchant programs referenced
text_array_fields.hint: Include named brands (CareCredit, Synchrony, etc.) and merchant/platform names; useful for frequency and network analyses.

text_array_fields.description: Short extracted claims or causal arguments (e.g., 'because X then Y', 'X leads to Y')
text_array_fields.hint: Extract explicit causal or conditional claims relevant to policy or impacts. Keep each entry concise.

text_array_fields.description: Free-text policy recommendations, suggested regulatory language, or concrete actions advocated
text_array_fields.hint: Capture short phrases or sentences of recommended policy actions (e.g., 'cap interest at X%', 'require FAP screening before offering financing').

text_array_fields.description: Extracted geographic (city, state, region) and demographic indicators when present
text_array_fields.hint: Include any location or demographic markers (city, state, rural/urban, race/ethnicity when explicitly provided) to support equity analysis.
